description: Enable code autocomplete/suggestions for the Code Project IDE editor. The sora-editor library already has EditorAutoCompletion as a built-in component. The existing JavaLanguage from sora-editor provides basic Java keyword completion. We need to ensure autocomplete is enabled and provide a richer completion source with common Android API classes/methods and Java keywords.
steps: Create `app/src/main/java/pro/sketchware/codeproject/editor/JavaAutoCompleteProvider.java` implementing sora-editor's `io.github.rosemoe.sora.lang.completion.CompletionPublisher` pattern. Specifically, create a class that can be integrated with the JavaLanguage or a custom Language wrapper. Since sora-editor's JavaLanguage already has built-in keyword completion, the simplest approach is: (a) Create a custom Language wrapper or use JavaLanguage as-is but ensure it provides completions. Actually the simplest approach: sora-editor's JavaLanguage already provides Java keyword autocompletion out of the box. We primarily need to ensure the EditorAutoCompletion component is enabled on the editor and add a supplemental completion source with common Android API identifiers.

Create the provider class that extends or wraps functionality. The sora-editor API for custom completions in version 0.23.5 works via the Language's `requireAutoComplete` method. Since JavaLanguage already implements this, the best approach is to subclass JavaLanguage as `JavaAutoCompleteLanguage` in `app/src/main/java/pro/sketchware/codeproject/editor/JavaAutoCompleteLanguage.java`. Override `requireAutoComplete` to call super (for keywords) and then add additional CompletionItems for common Android classes: Activity, Bundle, Intent, View, TextView, Button, RecyclerView, Toast, Log, Context, Fragment, etc. Also add common method names: onCreate, onResume, onPause, findViewById, setContentView, getString, getIntent, etc.

Modify `EditorUtils.java` to add a new method `loadJavaAutoCompleteConfig(CodeEditor editor)` that uses the new JavaAutoCompleteLanguage instead of plain JavaLanguage, and explicitly enables the EditorAutoCompletion component: `editor.getComponent(EditorAutoCompletion.class).setEnabled(true)`.

Modify `CodeProjectActivity.java` setupEditor() and openFile() to use the new `EditorUtils.loadJavaAutoCompleteConfig(binding.editor)` method for .java files instead of `EditorUtils.loadJavaConfig(binding.editor)`. Keep loadJavaConfig for backward compatibility with other parts of the app.
Ensure the EditorAutoCompletion component is enabled by default in setupEditor().
acceptance_criteria: Typing in the code editor triggers an autocomplete popup with Java keywords
Common Android API class names appear in the autocomplete suggestions
The autocomplete popup is functional (selecting an item inserts it)
verification: Run `gradle assembleRelease` from the project root and confirm it succeeds
findings: Build succeeds with assembleRelease (2m 16s). Used sora-editor 0.23.5 API: SimpleCompletionItem(label, prefixLength, commitText) constructor, CompletionItemKind.Class and CompletionItemKind.Method for item types. ContentReference.getLine() returns String. EditorAutoCompletion.setEnabled(true) required to activate the popup. JavaLanguage.requireAutoComplete called via super for built-in keyword completions.
